label: Melakhim Bet (2 Kings) — Part 2 — Chapters 8–13
summary_plain: The Shunamite woman returns from the land of the Philistines and her land is restored. Chaza'el murders Ben-Hadad and becomes king of Aram. Yehoram king of Yehudah does evil — but YHWH will not destroy Yehudah for the sake of Dawid. Yehu is anointed king of Yisra'EL by a prophet sent by Elisha. Yehu drives furiously. He kills Yoram king of Yisra'EL and Achazyahu king of Yehudah. Yehu executes Izevel — she is thrown from the window and the dogs eat her flesh as Eliyahu prophesied. Yehu destroys Ba'al worship from Yisra'EL — he gathers all the Ba'al worshipers into the Ba'al temple and kills them. Atalyah seizes the throne of Yehudah and kills the royal seed — but the infant Yo'ash is hidden. Yehoyada the priest crowns Yo'ash after seven years. Elisha is sick. Yo'ash king of Yisra'EL weeps over him. Elisha dies.
summary_scholarly: The ACR reads from DSS Kings fragments (4QKgs) and the Orit Ge'ez. Yehu's revolution (chs 9-10) fulfills Eliyahu's prophecy: YHWH's judgment on the house of Ach'av and Izevel. The execution of Izevel (9:30-37) — thrown from the window, trampled, eaten by dogs — fulfills Eliyahu's word exactly (1 Kings 21:23). Yehu destroys Ba'al worship but does not depart from Yerov'am's golden calves (10:29-31). Partial reformation is not covenant faithfulness. Atalyah's purge of the Davidic line (ch 11) is the single greatest threat to YHWH's Davidic covenant promise — one infant, Yo'ash, hidden in the Temple for seven years, preserves the entire covenant line. Elisha's death (ch 13) ends the prophetic succession from Eliyahu.

key_terms.definition: Son of Yehoshafat son of Nimshi. Anointed king of Yisra'EL by Elisha's messenger to destroy the house of Ach'av. Known for driving furiously (9:20). Kills Yoram, Achazyahu, Izevel, and all Ba'al worshipers. But does not remove Yerov'am's golden calves. Anglicized as Jehu.

Daughter of Ach'av (or granddaughter of Omri). Seizes the throne of Yehudah and massacres the Davidic royal family. The only reigning queen in Yehudah's history. Rules six years until Yehoyada the priest leads a coup and crowns Yo'ash (ch 11). Anglicized as Athaliah.

Son of Achazyahu. Hidden as an infant by his aunt Yehosheva in the Temple during Atalyah's purge. Crowned king at age seven by Yehoyada the priest. The single surviving heir of the Davidic covenant line. Anglicized as Joash.

fill_blank.prompt: The driving is like the driving of Yehu son of Nimshi, for he drives ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: The driving is like the driving of Yehu son of Nimshi, for he drives furiously.

fill_blank.prompt: Then Yehoyada brought out the king's son and put the crown on him and gave him the ______. They proclaimed him king and anointed him, and they clapped their hands and said: Long live the king!
fill_blank.source_quote: Then he brought out the king's son and put the crown on him and gave him the testimony. And they proclaimed him king and anointed him, and they clapped their hands and said: Long live the king!

multiple_choice.question: How was Izevel's death fulfilled?
options: She was thrown from a window and dogs ate her flesh, as Eliyahu prophesied
multiple_choice.source_quote: They threw her down, and some of her blood spattered on the wall and on the horses, and they trampled on her. When they went to bury her, they found no more of her than the skull and the feet and the palms of her hands.

multiple_choice.question: How was the Davidic line preserved during Atalyah's purge?
options: The infant Yo'ash was hidden in the Temple for seven years
A prophet smuggled them to Bavel
multiple_choice.source_quote: Yehosheva took Yo'ash the son of Achazyahu and stole him away from among the king's sons who were being put to death, and she put him and his nurse in a bedroom. Thus they hid him from Atalyah, so that he was not put to death.

faq.question: What does Atalyah's purge reveal about the vulnerability of the Davidic covenant line?
faq.answer: Atalyah massacres the entire Davidic royal family (11:1). One infant — Yo'ash — is hidden in the Temple by Yehosheva. The Davidic covenant promise — 'your throne shall be established forever' (2 Shemu'el 7:16) — depends on a single child hidden in a storage room. YHWH's covenant promise survived by a thread. This is the Great Eraser at its most extreme: the systematic extermination of a covenant lineage. The pattern repeats throughout African history. The Atlantic slave trade deliberately separated families, destroyed genealogical knowledge, and erased lineage connections. Colonial powers killed African royal families to end dynasties. The Herero genocide, the Zulu royal line's disruption, the Benin Kingdom's destruction — all follow Atalyah's pattern: destroy the seed, end the line. YHWH's response: the covenant survives through one hidden child. The line cannot be permanently erased.

faq.question: Why does Yehu's partial reformation matter for understanding covenant faithfulness?
faq.answer: Yehu destroys Ba'al worship — tears down the Ba'al temple, kills the Ba'al priests, ends Izevel's religious program. But: 'Yehu did not turn aside from the sins of Yerov'am son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin — the golden calves that were in Beit-El and Dan' (10:29). Partial reformation is not covenant faithfulness. Removing the most obvious idolatry while keeping the structural sin is the pattern of every reform movement that addresses surface symptoms while leaving root systems intact. European abolition of chattel slavery did not address the economic structures that continued to exploit African labor. Civil rights legislation addressed legal segregation but did not dismantle the wealth extraction systems. Yehu's reformation teaches: you cannot claim covenant faithfulness while preserving the golden calves. YHWH's standard is complete obedience, not selective reform.
